intro.title: API Reference
intro.pickScript: Pick a script in the top-right to substitute its real id into the examples below.
intro.authText1: Everything sits behind a single admin login. Management endpoints (files, async executions, listing, stop) require an admin session — the logged-in UI attaches it as a cookie automatically, or send
intro.authText2: The one endpoint meant for external systems,
intro.authText3: also accepts an issued API key via
intro.authText4: create and manage keys on the

mcp.title: Connect a coding agent (MCP)
mcp.desc: AgentFlow exposes an MCP server so Claude Code, Cursor or any MCP client can develop scripts on this instance directly: create/edit script files, set up the venv, run scripts and read the failing traceback — the full write→run→debug loop. Auth is an issued API key (create one on the /security page). Note: unlike /api/executions/run, the MCP endpoint gives an API key full script-management scope, not just run.
mcp.labelInstallSkill: Install the companion skill (teaches the agent the script conventions)
mcp.toolsExposedTrailing1: The same skill text is also available to the agent at runtime via
mcp.toolsExposedTrailing2: , so installing it locally is optional — it just helps the agent pick the right approach sooner.

runSync.title: Run synchronously
runSync.desc: Blocks until the script finishes and returns the final output_data. Best for external service-to-service calls — this is the one endpoint you can call with an X-API-Key (default timeout 300s).

uploadFile.title: Upload a file
uploadFile.desc: Admin only. Upload arbitrary binaries (PDFs, CSVs, images, etc.) that scripts can read by reference. Files persist until you delete them. Optionally tag with script_id for filtering.

runWithFile.title: Run a script with file input
runWithFile.desc: Pass a file reference anywhere in input_data using the marker {"$file": "<FILE_ID>"}. The execution engine resolves it before launch; your script receives an AgentFlowFile object in its place. Markers can be nested in objects / arrays.

listDeleteFiles.title: List / delete files
listDeleteFiles.desc: Admin only. Filter by script_id to show files tagged to a script plus untagged globals.

runAsync.title: Run asynchronously
runAsync.desc: Admin only. Returns immediately with an execution id; poll GET /executions/{id} or subscribe via WebSocket for logs. Better for long jobs and fan-out.

stopExecution.title: Stop a running execution
stopExecution.desc: Admin only. Force-stops the subprocess and marks the execution as cancelled. Always returns 200.

listExecutions.title: List executions
listExecutions.desc: Admin only. Newest first. Filter by script_id, status / trigger (comma-separated), and q — a free-text search that also matches inside log messages across runs.

monitoring.title: Metrics (Prometheus)
monitoring.desc: A Prometheus scrape target covering HTTP traffic, execution throughput / latency / the queue-wait→cold-import→script profiler split, LLM token spend, the warm-worker pool, delivery, and live DB/resource gauges. Secure-by-default: it takes the same API key as the run endpoint, a dedicated scrape token (AGENTFLOW_METRICS_TOKEN), or is fully open with AGENTFLOW_METRICS_PUBLIC=true.

pythonExample.title: Python example
pythonExample.desc: Anything that speaks HTTP works. Here's a minimal chat loop hitting /executions/run with client-side history.

contract.title: Script contract
contract.desc: What the platform passes in and expects back.

logging.label: Logging
logging.t2: . Structured logs go to the Logs panel and are persisted to the run history.

secrets.label: Secrets
secrets.t3: (keys are case-insensitive). Values are injected into the run's environment and never appear in your code, input, or the frontend.

httpHelpers.label: HTTP helpers
httpHelpers.t3: wrappers with sane defaults (timeout, follow-redirects, raise-for-status). They return the
httpHelpers.t5: straight through — so you don't re-write request boilerplate in every script.

fileInputs.label: File inputs
fileInputs.t4: at any depth. The script receives an

workingDirs.label: Working directories
workingDirs.t3: is this run's cwd (fresh each run, auto-pruned);
workingDirs.t4: persists across runs of the same script (good for caches, vector indexes, sqlite files).

asyncWebhook.title: Async submit + completion webhook
asyncWebhook.desc: The same /run endpoint, non-blocking: add wait=false to get the run id back immediately (also callable with an X-API-Key). Give a callback_url and the final result is POSTed there once the run reaches a terminal state; or poll GET /api/executions/{id} with the same API key.
asyncWebhook.labelCallback: webhook payload — POSTed to callback_url on completion
